title: Identifier/CURIE normalization policy (Bioregistry: HGNC/Ensembl/VRS/Mondo/ChEMBL)
context: Multiple sources use different identifiers for the same genes, variants, drugs, diseases, and pathways. A shared CURIE normalization policy is needed before multi-source integration so that all entities resolve to canonical identifiers and cross-source merges are auditable. Bioregistry is the chosen resolver. GA4GH VRS is the canonical variant identity standard.
objective: Define the canonical CURIE/identifier policy for each entity class: genes (HGNC/Ensembl), variants (GA4GH VRS + HGVS aliases), drugs (ChEMBL/RxNorm), diseases (Mondo/DOID), and pathways (Reactome). Document the Bioregistry-based resolution approach, alias retention rules, and precision requirements for variant merging.
acceptanceCriteria: Canonical CURIE namespace defined for each entity class: genes (HGNC), variants (GA4GH VRS), drugs (ChEMBL), diseases (Mondo), pathways (Reactome).
Source-native IDs are designated as aliases, not replaced, ensuring auditability.
Policy specifies that variant merges require human review; no silent auto-merge is permitted.
Bioregistry is designated as the resolver; lookup approach documented.
Resolution success target of ≥99% of entities (sampled) resolving to a live record is documented.
VRS key target of ≥95% of variant nodes carrying a canonical GA4GH VRS identifier is documented.
output: docs/identifier-policy.md defining canonical CURIEs, alias retention rules, Bioregistry resolution approach, and precision targets, committed via PR.
